title: The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace
content:
{{Top|rwm|leg|ref|italics=1}} {{Youmayd|the reference book|''The Phantom Menace''|[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (disambiguation)]]}} {{ReferenceBook |image=[[File:Art_of_Phantom.jpg]] |title=''The Art of Star Wars Episode I:
The Phantom Menace'' |author=[[Jonathan Bresman]] |cover artist= |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Del Rey]] |release date=[[September 7]], [[1999]]{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/ref/news19990914.html|text=''The Art of Star Wars, Episode I''}} |type=Hardcover |pages=224 |dimensions=12.2 × 9.4 × 0.7 inches |weight=2.9 pounds |isbn=0345431081 |series= |preceded by= |followed by= }} '''''The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace''''' features conceptual illustrations, sequential art, and fully executed paintings. This volume in the "Art of" series delves into the [[Lucasfilm]] archives, detailing [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|Episode I's]] use of traditional and high-tech media and exploring the creative impulse behind all of the designs. Inside are key action sequences, including the Podraces on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and the ground and space battles; interviews and photos of the artists at work; paintings that capture the exotic environments of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], Tatooine, and [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]; the visual evolution of the battle droids, the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] ships, and characters such as [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Queen Amidala]], and [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]]; and a showcase of poster art for Episode I. ==Contents== *Introduction *The [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] *The [[Planet/Legends|Planet]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] *[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]: [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] *[[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]: The [[City/Legends|City]] of [[Theed/Legends|Theed]] *[[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]: The Desert Planet *[[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]: The City Planet *From Sketch to Screen *Concept Design Team *Acknowledgments *From Movie to Marketing ==Continuity== *In the section at the end of the book highlighting each member of the Concept Design Team, it is mentioned that three of them ([[Iain McCaig]], [[Evan Pontoriero]] and [[William Beck|Bill Beck]]) were working on ILM's first all-digital feature film, ''Frankenstein'', during the writing of this book. This project was next mentioned on page 20 of ''[[The Making of Star Wars Revenge of the Sith]]'', where it is revealed [[Erik Tiemens]] and [[Ryan Church]] also worked on the project and that by that time it had been cancelled. Furthermore, the book ''[[Star Wars Storyboards: The Prequel Trilogy]]'' mentions that [[Derek Thompson]] also worked on this project as a storyboard artist. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace}} ===Editions=== {|class="wikitable sortable" {{Prettytable}} !
